---
title: "Ad-Hoc Revenue Data Check"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
server: shiny
---

<!--
  On-demand report for finance close. Nothing queries the database until the
  user clicks "Run now". Deploy to Posit Connect as an interactive (Shiny) doc.

  This version runs on a self-contained DuckDB database seeded with synthetic
  data, so it works with no external driver or credentials. To move to
  Snowflake, replace connect_db() and report_query (see the PRODUCTION note).
-->

```{r}
#| context: setup
#| include: false

library(shiny)
library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(DT)
library(glue)
library(writexl)
library(blastula)

# Row cap carried over from the GAI Snowflake guidelines: never dump raw data.
# The demo query is pre-aggregated so this rarely bites, but keep it as a guard.
ROW_CAP <- 500L

# --- Local demo database -----------------------------------------------------
# A DuckDB file seeded once with a fake FACTARR: one snapshot per month-end for
# the last 18 months, broken out by subscription / customer type / source.
DUCKDB_PATH <- file.path(tempdir(), "revenue_demo.duckdb")

seed_duckdb <- function(path) {
  set.seed(42)

  # Month-end snapshot dates for the last 18 completed months.
  firsts <- sort(seq(as.Date(cut(Sys.Date(), "month")), by = "-1 month",
                     length.out = 19))
  dates  <- firsts[-1] - 1                       # last day of each month

  customer_types <- c("Subscription Customer",
                      "Re-Occurring Usage Customer",
                      "Indirect Customer")
  sources <- c("CPQ", "NETSUITE ZAB", "EXCISE", "MYLODGE", "ZUORA INTEGRATED")

  n_subs <- 300L
  subs <- data.frame(
    SUBSCRIPTION_KEY = sprintf("SUB%05d", seq_len(n_subs)),
    CUSTOMER_TYPE    = sample(customer_types, n_subs, replace = TRUE,
                              prob = c(.70, .20, .10)),
    SOURCE           = sample(sources, n_subs, replace = TRUE,
                              prob = c(.60, .15, .10, .10, .05)),
    BASE_ARR         = round(runif(n_subs, 2000, 500000), 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  fact <- do.call(rbind, lapply(dates, function(d) {
    data.frame(
      REPORT_DATE      = d,
      SUBSCRIPTION_KEY = subs$SUBSCRIPTION_KEY,
      CUSTOMER_TYPE    = subs$CUSTOMER_TYPE,
      SOURCE           = subs$SOURCE,
      ARR_USD          = round(subs$BASE_ARR * runif(n_subs, 0.9, 1.1), 2),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
  fact$MRR_USD <- round(fact$ARR_USD / 12, 2)

  con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = path)
  on.exit(DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  DBI::dbWriteTable(con, "FACTARR", fact, overwrite = TRUE)
}

if (!file.exists(DUCKDB_PATH)) seed_duckdb(DUCKDB_PATH)

# --- Connection --------------------------------------------------------------
# PRODUCTION: swap this for the Snowflake connection, e.g.
#   DBI::dbConnect(odbc::odbc(), Driver = "Snowflake", ...)
connect_db <- function() {
  DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = DUCKDB_PATH, read_only = TRUE)
}

# --- The report query --------------------------------------------------------
# Same shape as the Snowflake version: leading commas, GROUP BY / SUM, and the
# "latest snapshot on or before the as-of date" pattern. The as-of date is
# BOUND as a parameter (?), never string-concatenated.
# PRODUCTION: point FROM at ANALYTICS_PROD_MART.MART.FACTARR + DIMACCOUNT join.
report_query <- glue::glue("
  SELECT
      REPORT_DATE
      , CUSTOMER_TYPE
      , SOURCE
      , COUNT(DISTINCT SUBSCRIPTION_KEY)  AS SUBSCRIPTION_COUNT
      , SUM(ARR_USD)                      AS TOTAL_ARR_USD
      , SUM(MRR_USD)                      AS TOTAL_MRR_USD
  FROM FACTARR
  WHERE REPORT_DATE = (
      SELECT MAX(REPORT_DATE)
      FROM FACTARR
      WHERE REPORT_DATE <= CAST(? AS DATE)
  )
  GROUP BY REPORT_DATE, CUSTOMER_TYPE, SOURCE
  ORDER BY TOTAL_ARR_USD DESC
  LIMIT {ROW_CAP}
")

run_report <- function(as_of_date) {
  con <- connect_db()
  on.exit(DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  df <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, report_query, params = list(as.character(as_of_date)))
  # Timestamp the run so the export records when the check was executed.
  df$DATE_RAN <- rep(format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), nrow(df))
  df
}

# Exact SQL that ran, with the ? placeholder resolved to the as-of date, so it
# can be copied and re-run against the warehouse to verify the numbers.
resolved_sql <- function(as_of_date) {
  sub("?", sprintf("'%s'", as.character(as_of_date)), report_query, fixed = TRUE)
}
```

## Run a report

Pick a report date (defaults to today) and click **Run now**. The query only
executes on click, so opening this page costs nothing. The date resolves to the
latest snapshot on or before it.

```{r}
#| echo: false
dateInput("as_of_date", "Report date", value = Sys.Date(), max = Sys.Date())
actionButton("run", "\u25B6 Run now", class = "btn-primary btn-lg")
textOutput("status")
```

```{r}
#| context: server
# Server logic. eventReactive => only fires when the button is pressed.
report_data <- eventReactive(input$run, {
  withProgress(message = "Running query\u2026", value = 0.5, {
    tryCatch(
      run_report(input$as_of_date),
      error = function(e) {
        showNotification(paste("Query failed:", conditionMessage(e)),
                         type = "error", duration = NULL)
        NULL
      }
    )
  })
})

output$status <- renderText({
  df <- report_data()
  req(df)
  sprintf("Returned %s rows for report date %s.",
          format(nrow(df), big.mark = ","), input$as_of_date)
})
```

## Output

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Results

```{r}
#| echo: false
DT::dataTableOutput("results")
```

### Verified SQL

The exact query that ran, with the report date filled in. Copy it into
Snowflake to reproduce these numbers independently.

```{r}
#| echo: false
verbatimTextOutput("sql_ran")
```

:::

```{r}
#| context: server
output$results <- DT::renderDataTable({
  df <- report_data()
  req(df)
  DT::datatable(
    df,
    filter  = "top",
    options = list(pageLength = 25, scrollX = TRUE),
    rownames = FALSE
  )
})

# Verified SQL tab: capture the resolved query on each run.
sql_ran <- eventReactive(input$run, resolved_sql(input$as_of_date))
output$sql_ran <- renderText(sql_ran())
```

## Export

```{r}
#| echo: false
downloadButton("download_xlsx", "Download Excel")
br(); br()
textInput("email_to", "Email results to (optional)",
          placeholder = "finance-close@company.com")
actionButton("send_email", "Send email")
```

```{r}
#| context: server
# Excel download of the current result set.
output$download_xlsx <- downloadHandler(
  filename = function() paste0("revenue_check_", input$as_of_date, ".xlsx"),
  content  = function(file) {
    df <- report_data()
    req(df)
    writexl::write_xlsx(df, file)
  }
)

# Optional email with the Excel file attached. Needs SMTP_* env vars set;
# harmless to leave unconfigured (it just reports the send failed).
observeEvent(input$send_email, {
  df <- report_data()
  if (is.null(df)) {
    showNotification("Run the report before emailing.", type = "warning")
    return(invisible())
  }
  if (!nzchar(input$email_to)) {
    showNotification("Enter a recipient address.", type = "warning")
    return(invisible())
  }

  # Bail out cleanly if SMTP isn't configured on this server.
  if (!nzchar(Sys.getenv("SMTP_HOST")) ||
      !nzchar(Sys.getenv("SMTP_USER")) ||
      !nzchar(Sys.getenv("SMTP_PASS"))) {
    showNotification(
      paste("Email isn't configured on this server (needs SMTP_HOST,",
            "SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS). Use Download Excel instead."),
      type = "warning", duration = NULL)
    return(invisible())
  }

  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")
  writexl::write_xlsx(df, tmp)

  msg <- blastula::compose_email(
    body = blastula::md(glue::glue(
      "Revenue data check for report date **{input$as_of_date}**. ",
      "File attached ({format(nrow(df), big.mark = ',')} rows)."
    ))
  ) |>
    blastula::add_attachment(tmp)

  tryCatch({
    blastula::smtp_send(
      msg,
      to          = input$email_to,
      from        = Sys.getenv("REPORT_FROM_ADDRESS"),
      subject     = glue::glue("Revenue data check \u2014 {input$as_of_date}"),
      credentials = blastula::creds_envvar(
        user        = Sys.getenv("SMTP_USER"),
        pass_envvar = "SMTP_PASS",
        host        = Sys.getenv("SMTP_HOST"),
        port        = as.integer(Sys.getenv("SMTP_PORT", unset = "587")),
        use_ssl     = TRUE
      )
    )
    showNotification(paste("Email sent to", input$email_to), type = "message")
  }, error = function(e) {
    showNotification(paste("Email failed:", conditionMessage(e)),
                     type = "error", duration = NULL)
  })
})
```
